nodes.context: Russian hacking group behind Dridex malware, active since 2009.
Ransomware group that Evil Corp affiliated with.
Botnet or group listed as author of Dridex in infobox.
Hacking group whose core member Vyacheslav Penchukov was a personal friend of Evil Corp leader.
Ransomware group that may have been confused with Evil Corp in a 2021 attack.
Worm whose infrastructure may be used by Evil Corp according to IBM.
Russian paramilitary organization; an Evil Corp member participated in a hackathon organized by them.

edges.evidence_quote: Between 2022 and 2024, Evil Corp diversified its tactics and began affiliating with other ransomware groups, including LockBit.
edges.context: Evil Corp affiliated with LockBit to use its ransomware.

edges.evidence_quote: Yakubets was a personal friend of core member or leader of the Jabber Zeus hacking group Vyacheslav Penchukov
edges.context: The leader of Evil Corp was a personal friend of a core member of Jabber Zeus.

edges.evidence_quote: IBM suggested that Evil Corp may be using Raspberry Robin infrastructure to carry out attacks.
edges.context: Evil Corp may be using Raspberry Robin's infrastructure for attacks.

edges.evidence_quote: analysts at Emsisoft suggested that a ransomware attack initially attributed to REvil may have been the work of Evil Corp.
edges.context: A ransomware attack attributed to REvil may have actually been by Evil Corp, indicating possible confusion or misattribution.

edges.evidence_quote: in December 2022, Igor Turashev and his company placed third in a hackathon organized by the Wagner Group
edges.context: An Evil Corp member participated in a hackathon organized by Wagner Group.
Necurs is listed as an author of Dridex, which is an alias of Evil Corp.
